search_queries_used: site:nice.org.uk NG127 numbness tingling sensory symptoms adults
rejection_reasons: Patient pages are not clinician guidance.
A sensory presentation does not establish stroke, cauda equina syndrome, neuropathy, or radiculopathy.
population_applicability: Partial: exact for adults aged over 16 years; the legacy workflow has no age restriction.
setting_applicability: Direct for primary or community recognition and referral, not autonomous diagnosis or emergency treatment.
UAE_applicability: International NICE guidance requires UAE clinician review and local emergency, imaging, and referral pathways.
recency_verification: The current official NG127 adult sensory recommendations, last updated 2023-10-02, were opened on 2026-07-13.
superseded_check: NG127 remains the current official NICE suspected-neurological-conditions guideline.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped adult sensory identity, distribution, onset, progression, associated weakness, bowel or bladder context, sensation, power, reflexes, gait, selected metabolic tests, urgent features, and clinician referral fields.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports sudden unilateral numbness, rapidly progressive symmetrical sensory change with weakness or imbalance, recurrent sensory episodes, distal sensory distribution, reflex and gait context, selected metabolic tests, radicular symptoms, bowel or bladder context, and clinician-led referral.

unresolved_source_gaps: People aged 16 years or younger are outside the exact source.

Trauma, skin changes, sensory level, coordination, cranial nerves, temperature sensation, combined B12/folate, imaging, prior notes, generic plans, analgesic plans, safety-netting, and fixed follow-up remain unsupported.
Diagnosis, investigation selection, urgency, treatment, and qualified clinician review remain clinician responsibilities.
